today_summary: GCM26 (Jun '26, DTE 34) trades $4,009.8 — pressing directly against the $4,000 put wall, the highest put-OI strike in the chain at 3,935 contracts. Wednesday's session saw put volume (7,912) outpace call volume (6,198) by 27%, confirming fresh defensive positioning rather than roll activity. ATM IV ticked to 22.42%, pricing a 1σ daily range of $56.6. Max pain stands at $4,800 — a $790 premium above spot, the widest divergence in the current 5-day window.
five_day_thesis: Spot surrendered $235.7 (−5.6%) over 5 sessions, crashing from $4,245 through $4,200, $4,150, and $4,100 before landing at the $4,000 put wall — which has been the dominant put-OI anchor on every single session this week without migrating even one strike. Net GEX in the ±25% window deteriorated from −137.68 to −217.92 (a −58% deepening), meaning dealer short-gamma exposure is accelerating: every incremental move down now forces additional hedging sales, amplifying rather than absorbing the trend. The gamma flip strike — the level below which dealers pivot to stabilizing long-gamma posture — receded from $3,400 to $3,225 over the same period, retreating in tandem with spot and leaving no GEX backstop above $3,225 in the current book. Despite the steep decline, ATM IV expanded only 0.36pp (22.06% → 22.42%), signaling the market priced a controlled drift rather than a panic — which means implied vol is cheap relative to realized moves if the $4,000 floor breaks. Max pain drifted just $4,850 → $4,800 (−$50) while spot dropped $236; the pinning gap of $790 is extraordinary and cannot close via expiry mechanics alone without a significant spot recovery.
what_changed: Spot fell $235.7 (−5.6%) over 5 sessions — every daily close was lower than the prior session
Net GEX deepened from −137.68 to −217.92 (−58%) — dealer short-gamma exposure at 5-day extreme
Gamma flip strike migrated $3,400 → $3,225 — stabilizing GEX backstop retreating below spot
Put wall at $4,000 held as top put-OI anchor all 5 sessions with zero migration — now directly at risk
ATM IV +0.36pp to 22.42% despite $236 drawdown — vol underpriced if $4,000 support breaks
Max pain gap widened to $790 above spot — largest divergence in tracked window, structural pin force weakening
prediction: The $4,000 put wall (3,935 OI) is the fulcrum: it creates real gamma-pinning pull but the surrounding GEX field (net −217.92 in the ±25% window) means dealers amplify any directional break rather than absorb it. A sustained close below $4,000 removes the primary put floor and opens a dealer-hedging cascade toward $3,900 (3,066 put OI) then $3,750. Conversely, a reclaim above $4,060 — approximately 1σ above the wall — would signal short-cover flows that could carry toward $4,125–$4,200.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot pressing $4,000 from above with net GEX −217.92 and put volume 27% above call volume. Bias flips neutral only on a confirmed reclaim of $4,060 with expanding volume.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Sell failed reclaims into the $4,010–$4,040 zone on rejection candles. Do not fade momentum directly into the put-wall bid — wait for price to fail at resistance before entering short.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade below $4,000 on volume triggers cascade toward $3,900 then $3,750. A reclaim and hold above $4,060 neutralizes short bias.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: +$56 above $4,000 (~1σ daily move). Above this level the negative GEX regime loses grip and a short-cover squeeze is underway.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 at $3,960 — take 50% off and move stop to breakeven. T2 at $3,900 (3,066 put OI wall) — exit full position.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: Net GEX −217.92 + ATM IV 22.42% → expect $55–$90 intraday swings. Size down 40% vs normal. The $4,000 level may produce sharp two-way spikes before directional resolution.
